name: @platform/tooling-ci
description: CI/CD pipeline definitions: workflow files, build scripts, security scanning, and deployment orchestration.

readme.summary: CI/CD pipeline definitions: workflow files, build scripts, security scanning, and deployment orchestration.
responsibilities: Define CI workflow files (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or equivalent) for build, test, and deploy
Run architecture governance checks as a required CI gate
Invoke container image builds via tooling-docker and infrastructure applies via tooling-terraform
Run SAST, dependency scanning, and container image scanning as CI steps
nonResponsibilities: Does not define what gets built (owned by individual packages)
Does not own deployment targets or runtime infrastructure (owned by infra-aws)
Does not own secret management for CI runners
usage: CI workflow files are picked up automatically by the CI platform on push
Local scripts can be run to validate workflow syntax before push
operationalNotes: Architecture governance (`orchestrator all`) must pass as a required check before merge
All secrets used in CI must be stored in the CI platform secret store, never in workflow files
